title: West Pharma says operations recovering after cyberattack
summary: West Pharmaceutical Services said on Friday its operations were steadily recovering after a cyberattack ‚Äãearlier this month disrupted manufacturing and logistics ‚Äåglobally.
thbAction: Use the article as an entry point to pitch THB's commercial visibility layer for territory planning, account prioritization, and next-best-action for pharma leadership teams.
contentHtml:
• Companies

May 15 (Reuters) - West Pharmaceutical Services

(WST.N), opens new tab

said on Friday its operations were steadily recovering after a cyberattack ‚Äãearlier this month disrupted manufacturing and logistics ‚Äåglobally.

The incident first detected on May 4 led the company to take systems offline and restrict access, which ‚Äãtemporarily disrupted operations.

Sign up here.

Shares of the medical equipment ‚Äãmaker were up more than 1% in ‚ÅÝafternoon trading, after a roughly 7% decline ‚Äãsince the incident.

Its high-value product sites, including its ‚Äãlargest facility in Eschweiler, Germany, are fully operational, with standard packaging and most West Vantage sites having resumed shipments, ‚Äãthe company said.

West did not disclose the ‚Äãfinancial impact or provide a timeline for full restoration, ‚Äåbut ‚ÅÝsaid the unauthorized activity had been contained and immediate risks mitigated.

Evercore ISI analyst Daniel Markowitz said he expects some impact to second-quarter results from ‚Äãthe cyberattack ‚Äãbut does ‚ÅÝnot anticipate any hit to its full-year outlook, adding that the ‚Äãeffect is likely already priced into the ‚Äãstock ‚ÅÝdecline.

Markowitz added that flexible capacity could help operations catch up within the quarter, implying potential upside.

Stryker

(SYK.N), opens new tab

‚ÅÝand ‚ÄãIntuitive Surgical

(ISRG.O), opens new tab

also reported cybersecurity ‚Äãincidents in March, while Medtronic

(MDT.N), opens new tab

disclosed an incident in April.
